entry.title: Re: CAP Java: How to connect to PostgreSQL on BTP from BAS
entry.content: @mioYasutuke: Were you able to connect from BAS to Postgre SQL DB and also fetch data via app itself? Or is it only possible to fetch data from local postGre? Our assumption is that after deployment to BTP and via app router link to app, to fetch PostgreDB data. In BAS, we get ResourceTimedOut issue, when requesting the endpoints.

entry.title: Re: Eclipse IDE (SAP BW/HANA) Problems In Linux
entry.content: Can you try with the latest openjdk? According the screenshot in the original post you are using open jdk 21. As far I can see the latest version is 23.

entry.title: Secure connection configuration in Employee Central and Employee Central Payroll in Grow with SAP
entry.content:
As we know that EC(Employee Central) and ECP (Employee Central Payroll) are interdependent  and they communicate with each other .Recently I got opportunity to  configuration connection between EC to ECP system on SAP on public cloud This blog post  describes step-by-step in how to configure connection with help of OAuth 2.0  which is the industry standard protocol for authorization.

The prerequisites:

1-Check, in which support package your service is available.

2-Parameter icm/HTTPS/verify_client is set to 1 (accept certificates) or 2 (require certificates).

3-Make sure that you’ve maintained the optional fields for System ID and API .

4-Activate SAP Gateway

5-Ensure that the Local Provider follows the format <System ID>_<Client>,

6-Check system alias for OData service id not then create it

Configuration step

1-Certificate generation

1-Login on EC system Go to Admin Center  Security Center and choose X509 Certificates

 

 

 

Choose Add to create a New X509 Certificate.

 

 

Please  create 2 certificate as per below naming format

<EC Instance Name>_<System ID>_<Client>_ADM

<EC Instance Name>_<System ID>_<Client>_ESS

Select SAP Cloud Root CA as Certification Authority (CA

 

 

 

Click on Generate and Save, to save your certificate key and download

 

 

 

2-Configuring OAuth Identity Provider

Login on ECP system and run t-code SAML2

 

 

It will open saml2 screen in new window click on trusted provider then Add   Manually

You need to add 2 trusted provider format should be as below-

<EC Instance Name>_ADM,

<EC Instance Name>_ESS

Choose next and Primary Signing Certificate and upload certificate which you have created in SC system and save it  you must perform activity for both provider which is mention above

 

Once both providers created then go to main screen and change it to

In the details section of your newly created Identity Provider, choose Add. In the Supported NameID Formats window, select Unspecified and choose OK.

In the details section of NameID Format "Unspecified", select Logon ID  or login alias as User ID Mapping Mode.If you are using SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication for Employee Central Payroll, then select Logon Alias as User ID Mapping Mode.

 

 

 

 

3-Creating Service Users

 

 

Go to transaction SU01 in ECP system and create following service users:

EC_ADM_OAUTH

 

 

 

 

 

EC_ESS_OAUTH

 

 

 

4-Registering OAuth Client

login on ECP system and rum t-code SOAUTH2 in OAuth 2.0 Administration  choose Create  create below 2

EC_ADM_OAUTH

EC_ESS_OAUTH

 In the Create OAuth 2.0 Client window, select a OAuth 2.0 Client ID, provide a Description and choose Next.

In the Client Authentication step, ensure SSL Client Certificate is checked and choose Next.

In the Resource Owner Authentication step, ensure Grant Type SAML 2.0 Bearer Active is checked. In the Trusted OAuth 2.0 IdP field, choose the identity provider you already created in the Configuring OAuth Identity Provider section and choose Next.

In the Scope Assignment step, add a OAuth 2.0 Scope ID, according to your client ID and choose Next:

Final screen will be as below-

 

 

5-Importing X.509 Certificates

Run strust t-code in ECP system and import previously create certificates from EC system in SSL server standard

 

 

 

 

 

6-Mapping X.509 Certificates with Table USREXTID

1 Run transaction SM30, open view VUSREXTID in edit mode.

2 Enter DN in the External ID type field.

3 Choose New Entries and import the certificate.

4 Provide following user names: EC_ESS_OAUTH

5 Set the Activated indicator to activate the client certificate logon for the user.

6 Save your entries and the assignment of External ID to Users will be displayed.

Repeat the same steps for user EC_ADM_OAUTH.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

7-Mapping X.509 Certificates with Table CERTRULE

1-Go to transaction CERTRULE.

2-Switch to edit mode and import your certificate in the Subject field.

3-Choose the Explicit Mapping button.

4- Select user EC_ESS_OAUTH and continue.

5-The new entries are displayed in the Explicit Mappings tab.

6-Save your entries.

Repeat the same steps for user EC_ADM_OAUTH.

 

 

 

 

 

Now you can check it at ECP system it will ask for EC system credential and authenticate from EC system

 

 

Reference-

ahttps://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_EMPLOYEE_CENTRAL_PAYROLL/185f14fbe60d4bbb8d7d5e4f8d89b24b/bb1f11be38134ae3aac6b3139297ffc5.html

entry.title: Data Transpose in SAP Datasphere using Union Operation
entry.content:
Introduction:

In our projects, we often encounter situations where the obtained data is in a different format than required. To align it with the desired structure, transformation logic must be applied. In this case, the incoming data has monthly values stored in separate columns, which need to be converted into a single value column with multiple rows for each period.

In SAC, this can be accomplished using the simple Transpose Columns to Rows action. However, Datasphere currently does not provide a transpose feature.

Example Scenario:

In this example we are using the COSP table from SAP ECC, where transaction currency values for each period are stored in separate columns. WTG001 for January, WTG 002 for February and so on.

Incoming data: SAP ECC Table COSP

Expected result:

Year

Period

Value

2023

001

4.17

2023

002

4.16

2023

003

4.17

2023

004

4.17

2023

005

4.16

 

Solution:

We can achieve the desired result in the datasphere using the union operation where the same table is combined with itself.

Steps to implement the same are given below:

Step 1: Load the data to a local table via replication flow.

The target table format in Datasphere, "IL_FI_COSP," will be an exact replica of the COSP table in SAP ECC. This table will serve as the source for the data flow.

Step 2: Create a dataflow with table “IL_FI_COSP” as the source.

In this case, the two input datasets for the "Union Jan Feb" are projections of the same table, IL_FI_COSP. In "Projection Jan," select only the fields relevant to January and create a calculated field "Period" to hardcode the value "001" for January.

 

Similarly, create a projection for February by taking only the relevant fields for February and perform a union operation between Projection Jan and Projection Feb.

Union Output columns:

In the union, include all source columns from the Projection Jan dataset as output columns. From Projection Feb, avoid adding the value fields (WTG002, WKF002 etc.) to the Union output columns. If they are added by default, select only those columns in the union and choose "Delete selected output columns." This is done to get the January and February values under the same column so that the output of the union will be:

Year

Period

WTG001

2023

001

4.17

2023

002

4.16

 

Map all columns from Projection Jan to the Union. For Projection Feb, map the February Period Value field to the corresponding field in Union as demonstrated below.

 

Step 3: In the same manner, create unions for March till December in the same data flow.

Step 4: Combine Union of January and February with Union of March and April.

Similarly, take all the columns from the first data set as the union out put columns. Map the relevant value fields from second dataset to the output columns.

Combine other unions too in a similar fashion till all the columns from January to December are merged.

Step 5: Once all the periods are combined, map the final union to the target table, mapping the value field to the final column (WTGBTR in this case)

Save, deploy and execute the data flow

Final Output:

 

Alternate Approaches

The same result can be achieved via SQL view or Python script. However, we chose the above method due to its better performance and the need to have the data in a local table for implementing additional business logic.

This approach can also be applied when transitioning from a key figure-based model to an account-based model.

entry.title: Re: RAP Unmanaged BO - Late Numbering
entry.content:
I am trying to return item key as response by following your code using late numbering .Below part works.

 cs_mapped-header = VALUE #( BASE cs_mapped-header ( %pid              = iv_header_pid
                                                          PurchaseContract  = iv_ctr ) ).

 This does not work.

 LOOP AT mt_item_buffer ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_item_buff>) USING KEY sorted_cid_ref WHERE cid_ref = <ls_header_buff>-cid.
        cs_mapped-item = VALUE #( BASE cs_mapped-item ( %pid                  = <ls_item_buff>-pid
                                                        PurchaseContract      = iv_ctr
                                                        PurchaseContractItem  = <ls_item_buff>-key-PurchaseContractItem ) ).
      ENDLOOP.

 Thanks,

entry.title: Creating 'Reset' button in SAC Stories
entry.content:
When working with the SAC reports, it is obvious ask from the users that they want to have a Reset button which could help them to instantly come back to the landing page of the story. At the moment, SAC doesn't have any such standard buttons/functionality available which user can bring in to the report and use for resetting all the values/filters.

 

However, with the use of scripting method we can achieve this! I'm going to share a glimpse of how to write a short script to get the reset button.

 

With the above screenshot, you can see that the 'Reset' button is placed next to other filters. The purpose of the button is to bring back all the filters to 'All' value or to the value which user wish to have and avoid manual resetting.

 

Code used for the Reset Button:

InputControl_1.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

InputControl_2.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

InputControl_3.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

InputControl_4.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

InputControl_5.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

InputControl_6.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

InputControl_7.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

InputControl_8.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

InputControl_9.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

InputControl_13.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

Another observation from the provided screenshot if you make is that we do have Monthly Detailed/Yearly Detailed and Hide Button. This Reset method is applied to these radio button group code as well where the tabular data is getting reset as well.

if(RadioButtonGroup_1.getSelectedKey()==="Value 1")
{
Monthly_TabStrip.setVisible(true);

Table_1.setVisible(true);

InputControl_10.setVisible(true);

Chart_20.setVisible(true);

InputControl_11.setVisible(true);

YTD_TabStrip.setVisible(false);

Table_2.setVisible(false);

InputControl_12.setVisible(false);

Chart_21.setVisible(false);

InputControl_14.setVisible(false);
}
else if(RadioButtonGroup_1.getSelectedKey()==="Value 2")
{
Monthly_TabStrip.setVisible(false);

Table_1.setVisible(false);

InputControl_10.setVisible(false);

Chart_20.setVisible(false);

InputControl_11.setVisible(false);

YTD_TabStrip.setVisible(true);

Table_2.setVisible(true);

InputControl_12.setVisible(true);

Chart_21.setVisible(true);

InputControl_14.setVisible(true);
}
else
{

Monthly_TabStrip.setVisible(false);

Table_1.setVisible(false);

InputControl_10.setVisible(false);

Chart_20.setVisible(false);

InputControl_11.setVisible(false);

YTD_TabStrip.setVisible(false);

Table_2.setVisible(false);

InputControl_12.setVisible(false);

Chart_21.setVisible(false);

InputControl_14.setVisible(false);
}

 

Happy Learning!

entry.title: 回复： 公司间销售业务的按单生产业务

entry.title: Re: No data was released for version Y10 and Consolidatio...
entry.content:
Hello Vanessa

We have a similar issue in GR onprem version 2020. The solution was to implement note:

3207472 - Correcting the BCF behavior related to consolidation unit attributes time and version dependent configuration - SAP for Me, which was mentioned in note

3144152 - Group Reporting: Balance carry forward of released data is missing - SAP for Me

Hope this helps (if it is also your GR version)

entry.title: Getting Started with SAP CPI: A Beginner's Handbook || Part 1
entry.content:
Contents Overview

  • What is SAP CPI?
  • Why is SAP CPI used?
  • Key Components of SAP CPI?
  • Key Benefits?
  • Sender and Receiver
  • Adapters
  • Types of Integration Process
  • Events
  • Synchronous and Asynchronous
  • Content Modifier

What is SAP CPI?

It’s a tool that facilitates seamless communication and integration between different applications system and data sources within an organization.

SAP CPI enabling Cloud, integrates data Cloud services, integration across on-premise and mobile applications by consolidating, processing and publishing data quickly and securely.

Why is SAP CPI used?

  • Cloud Integration
  • simplified connectivity
  • Data transformation and Mapping
  • Real-time Monitoring and Analytics
  • security and compliance
  • Scalability and Flexibility

Key Components of SAP CPI?

  • Integration flow Design
  • Pre-built Adapters
  • Monitoring and Administrator tools
  • Graphical Mapping tool
  • Deployment options

Key Benefits?

  • Reduced integration costs
  • Improved Productivity
  • Enhanced Agility and Analytics
  • Real time Monitoring

Sender and Receiver:

The sender represents the system that initiates the data transmission while the receiver is the system that receives the data.

The sender is the source of the message, and the receiver is the target system where the message is delivered.

The sender is connected to the start of the Iflow and sends data to the integration process. While the receiver is connected to the end of the Iflow and receives the processed data.

Sender is act like our outbound message and receiver is inbound message.

Adapters:

Adapters are used to connect different systems and applications.

HTTPS Adapter:  A lightweight adapter that can exchange data between systems over HTTPS protocols. It supports various message formats, including XML, JSON and plain text.

IDOC: The IDOC adapter enables SAP Cloud integration to exchange intermediate document (IDoc) messages with systems that support web communication via SOAP services.

OData: Allows users to communicate with an OData API using the OData protocol.

SFTP (Secure File Transfer protocol): The SFTP Adapter connects an Integration tenant to a remote system using the SSH (Secure Shell) file transfer protocol system to write files to the system.

Definition - SFTP adapter to encrypt and exchange sensitive business data between trading partners.

(RFC) Remote Function Call: It is a standard SAP interface that allows communication between SAP systems and other systems. It enables the exchange of data and execution of functions in remote systems. 

SOAP(Simple Object Access Protocol) Adapter: It allows you to exchange SOAP messages between remote clients or web services server and the integration server and supports security setting for signing and verifying the SOAP body.

  • SOAP Adapter is based on XML.
  • We can use SOAP Adapters to consume web services that are secured by a Security Token Service (STS).

When selecting the SOAP Adapter, it provides us with two options-

1) SAP RM ( Connect SAP system)

2) SOAP 1.x  (Connect third party application)

SOAP Processing Setting:

  1. Robust : If you want to get request and response. once you send the request you want the response then to go for robust.
  2. WS Standard: If you don't want to see any response to the client to go for the WS Standard.

WSDL:

Web Services Description language (WSDL) file for a SOAP Call, SOAP is the standard format for web services.

CSRF:

For fetch the token we’ve to use HEAD in postman.

HEAD

 API will be written here

Key

 Value

x-csrf

fetch

Why have we expose endpoint?

Exposing an endpoint allows external applications or systems to access and interact with an integration flow.

Types of Integration Process:

Integration process: To define the steps to process the message transfer between sender and receiver system.

Local Integration process: To simplify the integration process we can break down the main integration into smaller fragments by using local integration process.

Exception subprocess: To catch any exceptions thrown in the integration process and handle them.

Events:

Start Message and End Message: “Start Message” event marks the beginning of a message processing flow.      While an “End Message” event indicates the end of the flow, sending the processed message to the receiver.

Error Start and Error End: “Error Start and Error End” can be used only within an exception sub-process.

Timer Start: An integration flow to automatically start and run on a particular schedule.

Terminate Message: A Terminate Message event stops further processing of a message.

Synchronous and Asynchronous:

Synchronous: The message flow waits for a response from the adapter.

(Acknowledge receive to sender)

Asynchronous: The SAP application does not wait for a response from the adapter.

(Acknowledge does not receive to sender)

Content Modifier:

We use the Content Modifier step to modify the content of the incoming message by providing additional information in the header or body of the message.

Data type: java.lang.String

Headers: Headers can be access outside of the CPI. We can use headers to provide authorization.

Exchange Property: We can create property to use values within the Iflow.

entry.title: Re: Automatic OP clearing when using an alternative payer
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: S_PROGNAM Authorization check always successful
entry.content: Hey! I'd appreciate any help!

entry.title: S4HANA Public Cloud - CBO Upsert Error 'Expected type is 'Edm.Boolean''
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm trying an upsert to a custom CBO but the expected types are sort of strange.

 

It does not accept the values a GET request response.
Also numeric is not accepted as '1.00' but 1.00m

Example for the error:

https://xxxx/sap/opu/odata/sap/YY1_API_TEST_CDS/YY1_API_TESTSap_upsert?NumericID='0000000001'&SAP_Description='API-Test'&Amount_V=2.00m&Amount_C='Amount_C'&TextToChange='AV'&Quantity_V=9.99m&Quantity_U='G'&CheckBox=true

CheckBox = true is not considered as boolean, nor is 'true' or 'X' or 1 or whatever.

Has anyone solved this?

entry.title: Re: EDI MESSAGES FOR SUPPLIER CONNECTION
entry.content:
Hello,

Is this what you're looking for?

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3c916ef10fc240c9afc594b346ffaf77/70018c33e83f41dda431496b2eb77f8d.html?q=suppliers

If not, can you please provide more details what you want to achieve?

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: You cannot change the UoM group unless the new UoM group includes the set of conversion rules of the

Need guidance for this

entry.title: Re: Hi All - Where can i find a list of training and Cert...
entry.content:
Hello,

for learning journeys you can set a filter regarding the role:

Learning Journeys - Overview

There iss also a calender for the trainings, but there is no filter regarding user type. In the details there are informations for which kind of users the course makes sense. 

You should speak with your employer, because the have to pay the course.

entry.title: ALV report output screen will be blank.
entry.content: If we choose variant and execute the report , output will  be displayed if we stay in same screen , meanwhile if we try to switch tabs or windows to excel or any other work , output screen will be blank.

entry.title: Re: SAP Replication flow from SAP Datasphere to S3
entry.content:
For a replication flow to use initial and delta you need to enable delta capture on your local table.

You will need to create a new local table and set the toggle "Delta Capture" ON in the general table properties.

entry.title: SAP Business Accelerator Hub API's try out Sandbox Environment
entry.content: I am using equipment API try out option on sap business accelerator hub and in try out option sandbox environment is used so I just want to know whether API hosted on on-premise or they are completely on cloud.

entry.title: Re: Migration Cockpit: Archived migrated data should be m...
entry.content: But even if I delete the data with the Archiving Functions I'm not able to migrate the data again. The Migration Cockpit blocks this with the hint that the data was already migrated. What can I do to do the migration a second time after deleting the data using archiving?

entry.title: Re: Diagram of Time Management Objects and fields in SuccessFactors
entry.content: This is absolutely great! thanks, very helpful

entry.title: Re: Alerts not working after upgrade to version 10
entry.content:
Hi @JerushaLala ,

Have you installed the Service Layer? If so, is it up and running? Does the Workflow user have an assigned license? Is the password valid? These are things I would check. Additionally, you may try to use SAP's Guided Answer to troubleshoot this error:

SAP Business One Alerts are not sending - SAP Business One - Troubleshooting SAP Business One Alerts - Guided Answers

I hope it helps.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: Story Report for Performance Management - 360 Forms
entry.content:
Hi,

Thanks for sharing this documentation.

Do you confirm there is no standard Stories reports available for Performance & Goals?

We feel a little bit "orphan" without the standard Dashboards that were very easy to use, so that we hope having something ready-to-use for Performance.

Yes, basically ijwhost.dll is required in .NET (Core) to use unmanaged components.

Interestingly, according to Microsoft, "ijw" stands for "it just works"... 🤠

entry.title: Issue with Hierarchy CDS View
entry.content:
Hi 

I have created a CDS view with Hierarchy Node, But I get the below error when trying to activate the CDS view. 

Error - Annotation 'Hierarchy.parentChild' must be an array type 

@EndUserText.label: 'Generic Hierarchy Node'

@ObjectModel.representativeKey: 'HierarchyNode'

@ObjectModel: { dataCategory: #HIERARCHY }

@vdm.viewType: #BASIC

@Hierarchy.parentChild:

{ recurse: { parent: 'ParentNode', child: 'HierarchyNode' },

siblingsOrder: { by: 'HierarchyNodeSequence', direction: 'ASC' },

directory: '_Hierarchy'

}

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK

@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations:true

 

@ObjectModel.modelingPattern: #ANALYTICAL_DIMENSION

@ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities: [ #CDS_MODELING_DATA_SOURCE, #CDS_MODELING_ASSOCIATION_TARGET, #ANALYTICAL_DIMENSION, #EXTRACTION_DATA_SOURCE]

 

@ObjectModel.usageType.serviceQuality: #A

@ObjectModel.usageType.sizeCategory: #XL

@ObjectModel.usageType.dataClass: #MASTER

 

@analytics.dataExtraction.enabled: true

 

define view entity ZCDSI_DSP_HIERNODE

as select from hrrp_node_n

 

 

association [0..*] to ZCDSI_DSP_HIERNODETEXT as _Text on $projection.Hierarchy = _Text.Hierarchy

and $projection.HierarchyNode = _Text.HierarchyNode

 

association [1..1] to ZCDSI_DSP_HIER as _Hierarchy on $projection.Hierarchy = _Hierarchy.Hierarchy

and $projection.ValidityEndDate = _Hierarchy.ValidityEndDate //Reviewed

 

{

 

@Consumption.filter: {mandatory : true, selectionType : #SINGLE, multipleSelections : false }

@ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_Hierarchy'

key cast(hrrp_node_n.hryid as fis_hryid_prctr preserving type ) as Hierarchy,

@ObjectModel.text.association: '_Text'

key hrrp_node_n.hrynode as HierarchyNode,

@Semantics.businessDate.to: true

@Consumption.filter: {mandatory : true, selectionType : #SINGLE, multipleSelections : false }

key cast(hrrp_node_n.hryvalto as fis_datbi preserving type ) as ValidityEndDate,

hrrp_node_n.parnode as ParentNode,

@vdm.lifecycle.status: #DEPRECATED

cast( '000000000000001' as hryversn ) as HierarchyVersion,

@Semantics.businessDate.from: true

cast(hrrp_node_n.hryvalfrom as fis_datab preserving type ) as ValidityStartDate,

@vdm.lifecycle.status: #DEPRECATED

@vdm.lifecycle.successor: 'HierarchyNodeSequence'

concat(hrrp_node_n.hryseqnbr, hrrp_node_n.hrynode) as SequenceNumber, // do not use any longer, use HierarchyNodeSequence

hrrp_node_n.hryseqnbr as HierarchyNodeSequence,

hrrp_node_n.hrylevel as HierarchyNodeLevel,

hrrp_node_n.nodetype as NodeType,

hrrp_node_n.nodevalue as HierarchyNodeVal,

 

_Text,

_Hierarchy

 

}

where

hrrp_node_n.nodetype <> 'D'

and hrrp_node_n.hrytyp = '0000';

 



Thanks

entry.title: How to get all system status assigned to any application like equipment, Functional Location etc.
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

How can we get all system status assigned to any Application like Equipment, Functional Location, Notification, maintenance order etc.

entry.title: Re: DCD1 condition type is getting determined for US comp...
entry.content:
Hello,

The issue of the DCD1 (Cash Discount) condition type not getting determined for US company codes could be due to several reasons related to the payment terms configuration and master data setup. Here are some possible causes and solutions:

1. **Payment Terms Configuration**:
- Ensure that the payment terms associated with the preliminary billing document are correctly configured in the system. The CDS views for payment terms provide details about cash discount conditions and net due days. Check if the payment terms description and conditions align with the expected cash discount.

2. **Customer Master Data**:
- Verify that the payment terms in the customer master data are correctly maintained. Any discrepancy in the payment terms can lead to issues with the determination of the cash discount condition type.

3. **Condition Record**:
- Check if there is a valid condition record for the DCD1 condition type for the relevant combination of parameters (e.g., payer, document category, sales organization) specified in the billing document. The absence of a valid condition record could prevent the cash discount from being applied.

4. **Pricing Procedure**:
- Ensure that the pricing procedure used in the billing document includes the DCD1 condition type and that it is correctly configured to be determined based on the payment terms.

5. **Company Code Specific Settings**:
- Verify any specific settings or overrides for US company codes that might affect the determination of payment terms or cash discounts. Company code-specific configurations can sometimes alter standard behavior.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Inventory Management: Inventory Counting Process - Cycle Counting (IM-MM)
entry.content: Good job Dafina!

entry.title: Re: Issue with Hierarchy CDS View
entry.content:
The error "Annotation 'Hierarchy.parentChild' must be an array type" occurs because the annotation @Hierarchy.parentChild expects an array format,
but it may have been declared incorrectly in your CDS view.

Correct Syntax for a Hierarchy CDS View
Make sure that your CDS view follows the correct syntax for defining a hierarchy. Below is the correct format:
----------abap
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZCDS_HIERARCHY'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
@EndUserText.label: 'CDS View with Hierarchy Node'
@Hierarchy.root: 'ROOT_NODE'
@Hierarchy.parentChild: [{ parent: 'PARENT_NODE', child: 'CHILD_NODE' }]
@Hierarchy.level: 'HIER_LEVEL'
@Hierarchy.ancestors: [{ ancestor: 'ANCESTOR_NODE', child: 'CHILD_NODE' }]
define view ZCDS_HIERARCHY as select from <your_table>
{
key node_id as CHILD_NODE,
parent_id as PARENT_NODE,
hierarchy_level as HIER_LEVEL,
ancestor_id as ANCESTOR_NODE,
node_name
}

Key Fixes:
1. @Hierarchy.parentChild must be an array
- Use `[{ parent: 'PARENT_NODE', child: 'CHILD_NODE' }]`
- Incorrect: `@Hierarchy.parentChild: { parent: 'PARENT_NODE', child: 'CHILD_NODE' }` (missing array brackets `[]`)

2. Ensure Parent-Child Fields Exist
- `PARENT_NODE` and `CHILD_NODE` should be actual fields in your table.

3. Optional Annotations:
- Use `@Hierarchy.root` for defining the root node.
- `@Hierarchy.ancestors` can be used if needed.

Next Steps:
- Try correcting your CDS view based on the syntax above.
- If the issue persists, share your full CDS view so I can check it for further errors.

entry.title: Re: EDI MESSAGES FOR SUPPLIER CONNECTION
entry.content: Check also this blog SAP S/4HANA Cloud EDI Integration Strategy.

entry.title: Re: Automatic OP clearing when using an alternative payer
entry.content:
In SAP Public Cloud (FI-only), the Alternative Payer field in the Business Partner does not automatically trigger clearing when using payment advice.
To enable automatic clearing:
1. Define a Clearing Rule in Transaction OB74 to allow open item clearing between the customer and alternative payer.
2. Use a Customer Clearing Account (via F-30 or F-32) to link payments to the correct open items.
3. Enable BAdI Implementation (e.g., FI_PAYMEDIUM_SAMPLE_41) to enhance payment processing logic.
4. Ensure Alternative Payer is Assigned in the Payment Advice and Document Posting.
5. Check Automatic Clearing Configurations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud SSCUI settings.

entry.title: Re: You cannot change the UoM group unless the new UoM gr...
entry.content:
Hi @Underdog ,

SAP Business One requires that any new UoM group assigned to an item must include all the conversion rules present in the original UoM group. This ensures consistency in how quantities are calculated and avoids discrepancies in historical data. If the new UoM group does not replicate or extend the existing conversion rules, the system will block the change.

Looks like this is being applied even when the item is a brand new one, as you described. 

Additionally, have you attempted to replicate this scenario with another item, whether new or old?

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Business Calc Amount total 0 in Executive Review
entry.content:
In the client instance, we have labeled the standard Varpay column "Business Calc Amount" (goalResultsPayoutAmountGrandTotal) as "Financial STI Amount". Show totals is enabled in Display Settings.

When viewing a form, the total for Business Calc Amount shows fine. However, the total is always displaying as 0 in Executive Review (renamed to Consolidated view). Please see attached screenshots. I have already tried:

  • changing Executive Review populations: small group of users, view Team Manager, or All Employees. i've tried to filter out employees who are greyed out for bonus.
  • check the actual form: no issues in form view
  • save the Executive Review view

Note that the forms are all in Completed.

Is this expected behavior for Business Calc Amount and Executive Review? Other columns are displaying totals just fine.

entry.title: Dear Team, please, how can I enhance VIRAINV table to include VIRAINVITEM?
entry.content:
The problem is, the standard REPA520 - Correspondence: Invoice for Rent, is not exhaustive enough as it does not contain Document Number, Document Date, Posting Date, Transaction in Foreign Currency among other details which can be found in the RE Documents for Contracts report - REISREDOCCN. I have tried to merge VIRAINVITEM, VIRADOC, VIRADOCITEM and even FAGLFLEXA with VIRAINV table in my FSD to the Abaper but still not getting the desired linkage. Please, what can I do to get the desired details in the RECPA520 report?

Thank you, gurus!

entry.title: Re: Issue with Hierarchy CDS View
entry.content: Added the FULL Code . .can you check

entry.title: Re: Upgrading to Latest Goal Management – Cycle Planning & Preparedness – Best Practice Approach
entry.content:
@Tbrown 

  • Navigate to Report center
  • Click “New”
  • Click “Table”
  • Click Single Domain
  • Select “Goal Management” as the data you wish to start with
  • Define Team: Select any Team Manager and select “Other Filters” for the Team Reporting Type
  • Click on Data Sets and choose the Goal Template that needs to be converted, which contains the unsupported table fields
  • Click on “Columns” and select all columns configured for the Target and Tasks table
  • Click the Objective plan ID and Objective ID

Then through Import Performance Goals tool, in your csv import file, use ACTION: UPDATE to edit the goals' status value. Here's the help guide on how to import: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_PERFORMANCE_AND_GOALS/4a24e48b5c384b18a81f6b661305021e/7f279b4b774e41e1bb69594021577494.html

entry.title: Re: switching source systems from DEV to QA for BW dev
entry.content: Dev BW system is BW 7.5 and production is BW 7.0. Prod contains 14 years of data. in dev we want to push just 2 years of data

entry.title: Flexible Workflow - Agent Determination Rule Failed
entry.content:
Hello!

I have set up the flexible workflow scenario for Sales Orders and I want to use the team Functionality .

I set up the Teams and Responsibilities : 

and the respective sales workflows

My workflows are getting triggered ,but there's no agent determined.

You can see the error below:

Now , it seemed strange to me that the system can't find the agent responsibility rule ,so I digged in and find the standard rule ID for the workflow template

So , my guess is that this rule /FUNC/CMR1LVLA ,cannot be determined.

I used the fiori apps "Manage Responsibility Rules" and "Manage Responsibility Context"

In the first one , there is no /FUNC/CMR1LVLA in the list , which strikes me as unusual.

Also in the second one , there is no agent rule assigned.

So , is there something missing? 

I would expect to see the /FUNC/CMR1LVLA in the Responsibility Rules and Assigned to WS02000006 in the Responsibility Context.

Do I have to "install" these via a note or something?

Also , is there anything else I can check?

entry.title: Re: Mastering BDC Table Control: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content: Man, I saw this tag notification and I came here expecting the new Business Data Cloud... how disappointing! 😉

entry.title: How to add your functions to BAS -> Extend your SAP BUILD / BusinessApplicationStudio environment
entry.content:
You may have asked yourself how to build this functions that SAP is offering in SAP Build "New Project From Template" as for example: 

  • CAP Project
  • SAP Fiori Generator
  • ....

 

This is already and an advanced method.  Lets step back to the fundamental idea.

YEOMAN is a wide use code generator that helps you to generate files or changes content of files within your project. Fundamentals are explained on the YEOMAN page.

In SAP BUILD you can use YEOMAN in a terminal and type "yo":

With ARROW UP or ARROW DOWN you can navigate:

So you can do all kind of things as adding a HDB module to your project

If you are interest to build you own more complex wizards you can look at some SAP code as the CAP Project above:

https://www.npmjs.com/package/@sap/generator-cap-project

BR Volker

entry.title: Re: Mastering BDC Table Control: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content: Note that the term BDC used for "Batch Data Collection", has been obsolete for a long time and was renamed "Batch Input". You won't find BDC in the glossary of the ABAP Documentation, you will find Batch Input. ABAP Keyword Documentation

entry.title: Re: Print HU label at Product WT confirmation in S/4HANA...
entry.content:
You are right, that the print is generally for the warehouse order but since the warehouse tasks are a child of the warehouse order, the printing is also triggered during warehouse task confirmation.

If you set up the OPD to print for warehouse order status "D", then printing should be triggered for the confirmation WT 1-49, if you additionally set up to print for status "C", then printing should also be triggered for the confirmation of WT 50.

For the confirmation of WT 1-49, the form data provider will pass all open warehouse task. So for example, when confirming WT 1, the open warehouse tasks 2-50 will be passed to the form. To my understanding when confirming WT 1 you want to print for WT 1 - which is exactly the WT which is not passed to the form. To achieve this you can implement BAdI  /SCWM/EX4_OM_WHO_EXTEND_WT  to read the confirmed warehouse tasks for that warehouse order from CDS view I_EWM_WarehouseTask_2. For WT 1 the logic would be easy, because there is then only one confirmed WT. To make sure the logic also works for WT 2-50, it is probably easiest to sort the confirmed warehouse task descending by ConfirmationUTCDateTime.

I hope this helps.

Best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Re: Setting default date in smartfilterbar field not working
entry.content:
Do you want to set a fixed date? This is how we did this for the last week:

 var oDefaultFilter = {
                "createTimestamp": {
                    "conditionTypeInfo": {
                        "name": "sap.ui.comp.config.condition.DateRangeType",
                        "data": {
                            "operation": "THISWEEK",
                            "value1": null,
                            "value2": null,
                            "key": "createTimestamp",
                            "calendarType": "Gregorian"
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
            oSmartFilter.setFilterData(oDefaultFilter);

entry.title: Re: How to get all system status assigned to any applicat...

  • Run BS13 and in menu: go to, status control for any Object Type.

entry.title: SHD0 - Personal settings of ME52N transaction variant
entry.content:
Good day, 

 

We have a requirement to create a transaction variant for ME52N in SHD0. One of the requirements is to remove the "Personal Settings" button. However, we noticed that the personal setting (Item overview as grid control) in the actual ME52N is still affecting the variant. 

 

Is there a way to set a global setting for this so that whenever the user uses the variant, the "Item overview as grid control" is still unticked?

 

Or

 

Is there a way to set a default value for "Item overview as grid control"?

 

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: E-Kanban(PK13N/PK21) set Kanban Container on full with batch number
entry.content:
Hello,

SAP kanban with replenishment strategy for external procurement and batch managed components is currently not possible.

Error message is displayed, when the kanban status was set to full that a batch number must be insert for the batch managed component. There’s no possibility to select one entry or enter the batch number at the kanban board.

Kanban control cycle is created for material XXXX. Replenishment strategy external procurement 0007- Summarized JIT calls (w/o summary). Scheduling agreement is also defined for the vendor in the sap system.

Bill of material for different components (batch / none batch managed) is defined and released in plant XXX.

Stock of this materials is provided to the vendor and can be displayed with transaction ME2O.

With the goods receipt posting (set status green in the kanban board) of the produced material, the stock of the provided components needs to be reduced.

Goods movement of the material with movement type 543 will be done automatically with the goods receipt posting for none batch managed components, but for the other ones an error message is displayed

PK13N:

Material:3918792B not batch number

When you click the container and set to full->

Error Message:

CS03

Material: 3918792B

Material 39188019 needs a batch number.

entry.title: Re: qRFC inbound IDOC error not lock the queue
entry.content:
We found an enhacement spot that solved that: IDOC_INPUT_QUEUE_CONTINUE. 

On method INPUT_QUEUE_CONTINUE~QUEUE_CONTINUE you can control if the queue should continue or not with parameter RP_QUEUE_CONTINUE ('X' - queue should continue processing)

To know wich queue are you managing you have parameters IDOC_CONTROL (Control Record (IDoc)) and IDOC_QRFC_QUEUENAME (Name of tRFC Queue).

I hope it helps someone. Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Where I can set ,, ready to warehouse processing,, wh...
entry.content:
Good Morning @honzakraus,

In a Airport to Airport - Airfreight booking the destination location will be a port and not a shipper warehouse - hence you cannot set this Freight Booking to 'ready for warehouse processing'. If you create from the Air Freight Booking a Delivery Freight Order to transport the goods from the port to the shipper / warehouse location then you can set this Delivery Freight Order to 'ready for warehouse processing'

Regards,

Sara

entry.title: SAP 10 FP 2405 Server Resources
entry.content:
Experts,

Having upgraded several clients to SAP 10 FP 2405 MS SQL version we have noticed the resources maxing up and quite a bit of bloatware with this version. Both RAM and CPU are frequently maxing out. The server is slow and constantly freezes.

For example 16 GB RAM on a 4 Core server is maxing out frequently with not more than 3 users connected at a time. This gets worse with service layer activated. How else can we use the web client ?

I also cannot explain the extra services running - SAPMachine Binary, Apache Commons, Apache HTTP server - running and seemingly taking up resources. What is the optimal server spec required for running this version ? Screenshot attached with two users logged in. RAM is at 81% . RAM capacity is 16GB.

entry.title: Re: Timely Time Sheet Alerts for no records on previous working day

I just have one doubt when trying to replicate a similar scenario. I configured the alert rule and assigned it on Post Save on my mdf object. Then I run my job on IC and the process goes ok, with the alert rule being triggered. The problem is that I dont receive the email notification. I checked the trace and it looks okay "Rule 'XXXXX' executed with status 'SUCCESS'. 1 execute action(s) triggered". 

Any idea on what is missing for me to receive the notification? 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: fill Component list from maintenance plans work orders
entry.content:
Hello Josh,
You are referring to a standard functionality of SAP PM/EAM.
To have the components populated from the Maintenance plans, do the following 
(I assume all the config is there already)

1. Access the relevant maintenance plan.
2. Get the information, what Task List is used there.
It may also happen, you have a maintenance plan w/o a Task List. 
If this is the case, you need to create one and then go to the point 3.
3. Go to the Task List, operations list. Add components to the operations. 
4. Generate new Work Orders based on this maintenance plan. They will have operations & components assigned.

Alternatively: you can create ad-hoc Work Orders and still use these task list & components.
Once WO is created go to Extras - Task List Selection and you can assign those there. 


Cheers,
Dan

entry.title: Re: Hosting SAP Code jam events in Warsaw

Best regards,

Eberechukwu Nwaobiora

entry.title: Re: SHD0 - Personal settings of ME52N transaction variant
entry.content:
Hello @dperez57 

You can hide "Personal Setting" with ME51N and/or ME52N transaction variants.

Define ME51N transaction variant and deactive the "Personal Setting" function there:

 

Assign the transaction variant as the standard variant:

 

ME51N without the standard variant, the button is visible:

 

ME51N with the standard variant, the button is hidden:

If you need to restrict access to personal settings in purchase requisition processing, it is the easiest and the least intrusive method.

 

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Webinar: PwC Brazil & SAP - Brazilian Tax Reform 2025
entry.content:
Join PWC Brasil in partnership with SAP for an insightful webinar dedicated to exploring the complexities and opportunities of the Tax Reform in Brazil. 

Reasons to attend:
- Gain an understanding of the main challenges and opportunities during this transitional tax period.
- Learn from PWC Brasil and SAP experts about the latest Technical Notes and their significance.
- Discover strategies to manage potential impacts on your IT and business processes.

Whether you are in finance, IT, or business operations, this session will provide actionable information to help you navigate the upcoming changes.

Speakers:
Carlos Pedral, Partner, PwC Brasil
Kleber Romano, Partner, PwC Brasil
Barthel Knut, Head of Localization Product Management Latin America, SAP
Maria Izilda Bianco Serrano, SAP Brazil Product Manager, SAP

Seats are limited, so secure your spot by registering now!

If you want to get more information about the Brazilian Tax Reform provided by SAP experts, join the Brazil - Tax Reform Localization Work Zone group.

Your help would be incredibly valuable, and I’d be happy to exchange ideas on SAP-related topics as well. Looking forward to learning from this amazing community!

entry.title: Re: You cannot change the UoM group unless the new UoM gr...
entry.content: Yes , in other items also it is showing and the conversion is also defined in UOM group

entry.title: Re: SAP IBP Keyfigure value for last day of the month
entry.content:
Hi @victor_hugodebastosleo ,

 

This is what the IBP_LPA function does behind the scenes : it finds the last period and it aggregates the values. Unfortunately it is not useful in this case.

There is a manual solution to display the last day of month: you will need to create a new attribute (integer type), assign it to time profile, update the time profile and then display it within a KF :

1. Create a new attribute (ZLASTDAY) of Integer type.

2. Browse the time profile used by the Planning Area -> edit -> select the Day level ->  Assign Attribute (ZLASTDAY). Check and activate the time profile

3. Data integration -> download the Time Profile with existing time periods -> populate the new column (ZLASTDAY) - this is where you will manually have to enter a value ("1") for each last day of the month. 

4. Upload the new time profile CSV.

5. Head over to the Planning Area -> browse the Planning Levels with Day (eg DAYPRODLOCCUST) -> Edit -> Change Assignment -> Select ZLASTDAY attribute. Check and Activate the PA

6. Create a new KF (ZLASTDAYOFMONTH) @ DAYPRODLOCUST = IF(ISNULL("ZLASTDAY"),0,1) - in the additional inputs you will need to add a KF populated at this level (eg Actualsqty). Check and Activate the PA

7. Display the new KF (ZLASTDAYOFMONTH) in the PA .

entry.title: SAP Document Reporting Compliance for Mauritius & Jordan
entry.content:
Mauritius: Simplifying Tax Reporting

Mauritius has a comprehensive tax system that requires businesses to file various reports, including VAT returns, corporate tax returns, and recently eInvoicing through the Mauritius Revenue Authority (MRA). With DRC, companies can streamline the entire reporting process by automating data collection, validation, submission and the “final mile” to the customer.

Key Benefits of DRC in Mauritius:

  • Automated eInvoice Generation: The solution generates eInvoices in the required formats, eliminating manual effort and reducing the risk of errors.
  • Audit Trail and Compliance Monitoring: DRC maintains a complete audit trail, enabling companies to track changes and demonstrate compliance during audits.
  • Localized Content: Through SAP DRC Extensibility we create local content packages for Mauritius, ensuring that you comply with the latest regulatory requirements.

Jordan: Enhancing Tax Transparency

Jordan has implemented various tax reforms in recent years, emphasizing transparency and accurate reporting. DRC can help businesses in Jordan meet these stringent requirements while minimizing the administrative burden as the Jordanian government rolls out its eInvoicing system.

Key Benefits of DRC in Jordan:

  • Electronic Submission: The solution supports electronic submission of tax returns, streamlining the submission process and reducing paper-based documentation.
  • Scalability: DRC can handle large volumes of data, making it suitable for businesses of all sizes operating in Jordan.

By implementing SAP DRC, companies in Mauritius and Jordan can significantly reduce the time and effort required for tax compliance while minimizing the risk of penalties and fines.

Our DRC Extensibility Template allows us to customize the functions and features as you require for your business scenario’s:

Mauritius: Live across ECC, S/4HANA, S/4 Private Cloud

  • Out of the box iFlow to the MRA
  • Flexible AIF Tables to map your data fields
  • Unique IRN Number stored in SAP
  • QR Code
  • Standard DRC Configuration

Utilizing our DRC template means your business has a solution already being used by multiple clients in Mauritius which to users is no different to any other DRC Country solution provided by SAP:

Jordan: Live across ECC, S/4HANA & S/4 Private Cloud (Live since the pilot began 2 years ago)

  • Out of the box iFlow to ISTD
  • Flexible AIF Tables
  • QR Code
  • Standard DRC Configuration

As with all DRC solutions all files are stored directly in DRC for users:

While DRC offers numerous benefits, implementing and configuring the solution can be a complex process, especially for businesses with unique requirements or operating in multiple jurisdictions. This is where CrimsonSAP can provide invaluable assistance.

We have a team of experienced consultants who specialize in DRC implementations and customization. We are currently helping multiple organisations on their eInvoicing journey in Mauritius and Jordan to navigate the complexities of the solution, ensuring a smooth and efficient deployment process.

  • Analysis: Our consultants will work closely with your team to understand your specific eInvoicing needs and regulatory requirements and customize our DRC solution to your organization.
  • Solution Design and Configuration: Based on the analysis, they will install our DRC extensibility template and configure DRC to meet your business requirements, including customizations and integrations with other systems.
  • User Training and Support: The team will provide knowledge transfer and training, ensuring you can effectively use and maintain the DRC extensible solution.
  • Ongoing Maintenance and Updates: As tax regulations evolve, we will help you stay up-to-date with the latest changes and ensure your DRC solution remains compliant.
  • Client success stories on linkedin coming soon

With our expertise and localized content, our DRC extensibility template empowers businesses to focus on their core operations while ensuring seamless eInvoicing integration with the tax authority and importantly your customers.

For multinational organizations already using SAP's Document Reporting Compliance (DRC) solution, Crimson offers a powerful way to extend and customize the platform to meet their unique global reporting needs.

In our next blogs we will explore the Baltics, Balkans, CIS and our experience in Malaysia and other full countries upcoming.

entry.title: CPI ftp / iflow: how to start post processing after ftp transfer of multiple files is completed
entry.content:
Hi all,

newb question: We need to transfer several files via ftp and after all files have been received trigger a job in the SAP ERP system. How can I achieve this within an iflow ? 
Start of the iflow can either be the ftp poll or any other trigger from SAP ERP.

Could anyone give me hint how to implement this ?
Thanks

Karsten

entry.title: Aging Report for Accounts Payable not working
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are encountering an issue when attempting to open the Aging Report for Accounts Payable app. The following error message appears:

"It seems that your profile is not configured for the system.
Contact your system administrator or end the current session and try again later.
Notify the system administrator
End session"

Despite having the necessary role assigned, we are still unable to access the app. Could you please advise on the possible cause of this issue and any recommended steps for resolution?

Thank you for your support.

entry.content: Hello, do we have any update on this please?

entry.title: Re: SHD0 - Personal settings of ME52N transaction variant
entry.content: Thank you for this, Dominik. However, according to business, some users can also access ME52N (hence, they can also access the "Item overview as grid control" setting). Issue is that, they may be a chance where a user (with access to ME52N and has ticked "Item overview as grid control"), goes to the variant and can edit fields that are supposed to be output only. We also cannot set the fields as output only if "Item overview as grid control" is ticked according to this SAP note: https://me.sap.com/notes/1885057

entry.title: Re: Issue with Hierarchy CDS View
entry.content:
The error message "Annotation 'Hierarchy.parentChild' must be an array type" suggests that the annotation @Hierarchy.parentChild is incorrectly formatted.
In CDS views, annotations that expect an array should be enclosed in square brackets [].

Issue in Your Code:Currently, your annotation is written as:

@Hierarchy.parentChild: {
recurse: { parent: 'ParentNode', child: 'HierarchyNode' },
siblingsOrder: { by: 'HierarchyNodeSequence', direction: 'ASC' },
directory: '_Hierarchy'
}
This is incorrect because @Hierarchy.parentChild expects an array of structures, not a single structure.

Corrected Code:

Modify the annotation to wrap the structure inside square brackets []:
@Hierarchy.parentChild: [
{
recurse: { parent: 'ParentNode', child: 'HierarchyNode' },
siblingsOrder: { by: 'HierarchyNodeSequence', direction: 'ASC' },
directory: '_Hierarchy'
}
]
Why This Works?
The @Hierarchy.parentChild annotation expects an array (i.e., multiple hierarchy definitions can be provided).
The corrected version ensures that the value is inside a list ([]), making it syntactically correct.

Next Steps:
Update the annotation as shown above.
Save and activate the CDS view.
If any further issues arise, check related annotations and ensure all hierarchy-related annotations align correctly.Let me know if you need further clarifications

entry.title: Re: RAP Node Extensibility for Unmanaged BO
entry.content: Hello Michael,

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to detect whether a BAPI is for read o...
entry.content:
I would say, the answer is a simple "no". (As outlined by the insightful comments above.)

Both types of BAPIs (read and write BAPIs) usually have input as well as output parameters, so there is no way to tell from the metadata alone.

entry.title: Re: RAP Node Extensibility for Unmanaged BO
entry.content: Hello Michael, I have checked the CDS root view R_SalesOrderTP, and I noticed that 'allowNewCompositions: true' is missing in the extensibility section. Does this mean we cannot add new nodes?

entry.title: Re: how to restrict serial numbers at plant level in ewm
entry.content:
Hi Sachin,

Sorry I am away from system.

-> Create new available group, Stock type....etc

-> Then assign EWM-ERP interface Plant+Storagelocation combination Assign availability group....

-> Check PED/Owner need to change ?

Hope it clears. Please let us know further questions.

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: Check when deleting master data like customers and pr...
entry.content:
Before deleting the Master Data you can perform a preliminary check in "Manage Planning Objects" app. 

Say for example you want to delete the Product = PROD 1, you can check if there are any plobj created for it, like below:

entry.title: Re: Aging Report for Accounts Payable not working
entry.content:
Have you actually the SAP_BR_AP_MANAGER role required to execute Aging Report for Accounts Payable, also check other requirements.

Also look for/check KBA such as 3305548 - How to resolve "It seems your profile is not configured for this System. Please contact your system administrator or end this session and try again later" error in Embedded SAC? or similar KBA for your exact status.

entry.title: Re: Fiori My Inbox no Details
entry.content:
Hello Chouhan

I have encountred the same problem but I did not manage to have access to the link you shared. Can you share with me me the procedure step by step please ?

Thank you !

Soufiane

entry.title: Re: Does IAS need any extra configuration or configuratio...
entry.content: Hi, how was this solved?

entry.title: Issue with "Preview on Device" Progress in SAP Build Learning Path
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working through the SAP Build learning path, and I have successfully completed the "Preview on Device" step as instructed. However, my learning status remains at 80%, and this step is not marked as completed.

Since the next tasks are locked until this step is recognized as done, I am unable to proceed further. I have tried repeating the process multiple times, but the issue persists.

Has anyone else experienced this problem? Is there a way to manually mark the step as completed or refresh the progress?

Any help would be greatly appreciated!

Best regards, Mazen

SAP Build

entry.title: Re: Eclipse(HANA Development): Error when searching for a...
entry.content:
My case is a bit different, but I ended up with the same problem and found a way to solve it.

As has been mentioned, version 2024-12 is not supported, so you should install one of the supported ones (the latest being 2024-9). The issue is that I made a mistake and installed version 2024-12, which gave me this error. From that point on, I spent three weeks trying every imaginable way to uninstall Eclipse and reinstall different versions, but the error persisted.

I analyzed the error log and found on Stack Overflow that the problem is caused by the presence of two plugins with different versions:

org.apache.commons.commons-logging_1.3.4.jar
org.apache.commons.logging_1.2.0.v20180409-1502.jar

They are located in the ..\eclipse\plugins folder.

Since Eclipse is self-executable, managing libraries is straightforward. I renamed the more recent one—without much hope, I might add—and to my surprise, it works correctly.

I understand that the definitive solution should come from SAP, but in the meantime, this can serve as a workaround. I did this a few days ago, and so far I haven’t encountered any other error or problem.

Try it and see if you’re lucky.

entry.title: Re: You cannot change the UoM group unless the new UoM gr...
entry.content:
Hi,

You can refer the SAP Note 2634720 - Rules for changing UoM relationship in UoM group definition

https://me.sap.com/notes/2634720/E

Hope this helps,

SonTran

entry.content: HI,
